gotchas_tested: merge uses 'variable' not 'value' in data_to_merge
makeData variables must be resolved object (via $var to merge output)
query extracts from $var reference correctly
evaluation has both success AND failure transitions
hex-only task IDs (a1a1, b2b2, c3c3, d4d4, e5e5, f6f6)
makeData uses <!var!> syntax for substitution

test2_childjob_loop.pattern: childJob with data_array -> query loop -> query [**].taskStatus
gotchas_tested: childJob actor is 'job' not 'Pronghorn'
childJob task is '' (empty string)
childJob job_details outgoing is null
childJob variables is {} when using data_array (loop mode)
loopType 'parallel' for simultaneous execution
All unused incoming fields present (transformation: '', etc.)
query uses flat variable names for childJob output
query '[**].taskStatus' extracts from all loop iterations

test3_adapter_servicenow.pattern: merge body -> adapter create -> query response -> extract ID
gotchas_tested: adapter app from apps.json ('Servicenow' not 'ServiceNow')
adapter_id in incoming variables (not in schema, must add manually)
error transition on adapter task (state: 'error')
$var not inside nested body object (uses $var.mergeId.merged_object)
JSON duplicate key workaround (error handler intermediate task)
merge builds body object since $var doesn't resolve inside nested objects

assertions.test: T3: error transition caught adapter failure

test3_adapter_servicenow.notes: ServiceNow adapter returned an error (likely auth/field validation), but the error transition correctly caught it and routed to the error handler task. This validates the error handling pattern works.

error_handling: intermediate task for duplicate key workaround
